question_text: A block of copper has a mass of 200 g and a volume of 22.4 cm³. What is the density of copper?
explanation: Density is calculated using the formula: Density = Mass ÷ Volume. Here: 200 g ÷ 22.4 cm³ = 8.93 g/cm³. This is close to the actual density of copper, showing it is a dense solid metal.
